name: #{taskYarnInstallerName}#
description: Installs modern Yarn through Corepack by default, or Yarn Classic on request
helpMarkDown:
[Documentation](https://github.com/DownAtTheBottomOfTheMoleHole/yarn-ado#quick-start) (Version #{Version}#).

By default this task provisions the latest stable modern Yarn release through Corepack. Requests for Yarn Classic 1.x use official releases from yarnpkg/yarn.
author: #{author}#

inputs.name: versionSpec
inputs.label: Version of Yarn to install
inputs.helpMarkDown: A version or version range that specifies the Yarn version to make available on the path. The default `stable` value activates the latest stable modern Yarn release through Corepack. Requests for Yarn 1.x use official Yarn Classic releases. Use x as a wildcard. See the [list of available Yarn Classic releases](https://github.com/yarnpkg/yarn/releases).

inputs.name: checkLatest
inputs.label: Always download the latest matching version
inputs.helpMarkDown:
For Yarn Classic requests, always check for and download the latest available version of Yarn which satisfies the version spec. This option will also always incur download time, even if the selected version of Yarn is already cached. For Yarn 2+ requests resolved through Corepack, the version spec itself controls which release is activated.

Enabling this option could cause unexpected build breaks when a new version of Yarn is released.

inputs.name: includePrerelease
inputs.label: Include prereleases
inputs.helpMarkDown: If set, Yarn Classic prereleases will be included when resolving 1.x versions. We recommend you don't set this unless you need it and you have a more restrictive version pattern such as ex: 1.7.x. This parameter does not affect explicit version patterns such as 1.7.1, and Yarn 2+ requests should specify the desired prerelease directly in versionSpec.
